--- title: Demonstration --- ::: {#00923a9b .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} from IPython import get_ipython ``` ::: This file is used to demonstrate interacting with Jupyter notebooks in * VS Code * PyCharm Professional * Jupyter notebook server This code is used to illustrate the behavior of each of the platforms and. It is not necessary to understand the code itself at the beginning of the course. The introduction will provide explanation of code used here. ::: {#25dd42cf .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} import numpy as np x = 2 * np.pi print(f"The value of 2π is {2 * np.pi}") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` The value of 2π is 6.283185307179586 ``` ::: ::: ::: {#4e558840 .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} import pandas as pd df = pd.read_csv("data/momentum.csv", parse_dates=True, index_col="date") df.head() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ```{=html}

You can also have code blocks. ```python for i in range(10): print(i) ``` Markdown cells also support both inline math, $\alpha+\beta=\gamma$ or entire lines, $$ f(x) = \frac{1}{2\pi}\exp\left(-\frac{x^2}{2}\right),$$ using $\LaTeX$ code.
